title: Lysogeny Broth (LB) medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"322a6","text":"Lysogeny broth (LB) is a nutritionally rich medium which is primarily used for the growth of bacteria[1]. The initialism is also commonly, albeit incorrectly, taken to mean Luria broth, Lennox broth, or Luria-Bertani medium. According to its creator Giuseppe Bertani, the abbreviation LB was actually intended to stand for lysogeny broth. The formula of the LB medium was","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":173,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":186,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":203,"length":20},{"style":"bold","offset":285,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":323,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":358,"length":9},{"style":"sup","offset":101,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":101,"length":3}],"data":{}},{"key":"f9016","text":"published in 1951 in the first paper of Bertani on lysogeny[2]. There are several common formulations of LB. Although they are different, they generally share a somewhat similar composition of ingredients used to promote growth, including the following: Peptides and casein peptones, Vitamins (including B vitamins), Trace elements (e.g. nitrogen, sulfur, magnesium) and Minerals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":59,"length":3},{"style":"bold","offset":105,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":59,"length":3}],"data":{}},{"key":"6juaj","text":"Sodium ions for transport and osmotic balance are provided by sodium chloride (NaCl). Tryptone is used to provide essential amino acids such as peptides and peptones to the growing bacteria, while the yeast extract is used to provide a plethora of organic compounds helpful for bacterial growth. These compounds include vitamins and certain trace elements.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":114,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"9E3B94468D9811ED966F0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1128/JB.186.3.595-600.2004"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D616FE908D9811EDB90F3B5D39B31490","url":"https://doi.org/10.1128/jb.62.3.293-300.1951"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2cfdj","text":"Follow step by step, unless stated otherwise. Equipment needed should be standard to a microbiology lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ft4jq","text":"Prepare glassware by cleaning it, and ensure that scale is sufficiently calibrated","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1rsrb","text":"When removing autoclaved components, be sure to take care as this can be very hot. If using antibiotics, use sufficient PPE to protect yourself, as some can be toxic to humans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3gq02","text":"Analytical scale, autoclave, bottle, weight vessel, LAF bench","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: All compounds are measured using a high precision analytical scale from powdered compounds. Each compound is measured to within 1% of the target weight. All compounds are mixed in a Duran bottle
Fill the bottle with 400 mL double-distilled water

Measure 5000 mg Tryptone, 2500 mg Yeast extract and 2500 mg Sodium chloride Powdered compounds: Tryptone Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #T9410 Yeast Extract Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #Y0875 Sodium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S9625
Add powdered solids into bottle, and use a magnetic mixer with a stir bar to mix for 00:05:00
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 6.7 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Add distilled water to a total of 500 mL
Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00 Cool to 50°C and supplement with antibiotics as appropriate
All compounds are measured using a high precision analytical scale from powdered compounds. Each compound is measured to within 1% of the target weight. All compounds are mixed in a Duran bottle
Fill the bottle with 400 mL double-distilled water

Measure 5000 mg Tryptone, 2500 mg Yeast extract, 2500 mg Sodium chloride and 7500 mg agar Powdered compounds: Tryptone Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #T9410 Yeast Extract Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #Y0875 Sodium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S9625 Agar Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A1296 (CAS: 9002-18-0)
Add powdered solids into bottle, and use a magnetic mixer with a stir bar to mix for 00:05:00
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 6.7 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Add distilled water to a total of 500 mL
Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00 Cool to 50°C and supplement with antibiotics as appropriate Agar can be stored, then reheated to 50°C to be poured
creator: Andreas Sagen
